asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline

description: Switched serde_json_serialize_profile to use simd-json (produces identical output, ~24% faster)

asi.notes: simd-json produces byte-for-byte identical output to serde_json for BlueskyProfile. Already used for serialization in redis.rs and sqlite.rs.

description: Confirmed simd-json for BlueskyProfile serialization (~25% improvement)

asi.hypothesis: Using simd-json for BlueskyProfile serialization instead of serde_json

description: Final confirmation: simd-json for BlueskyProfile serialization (~25% improvement, consistent across runs)

asi.hypothesis: Using simd-json for BlueskyProfile serialization

description: Added skip_serializing_if to more BlueskyProfile fields - 50% faster for minimal profiles

asi.hypothesis: Adding skip_serializing_if to more fields
asi.notes: Full profile unchanged (all fields Some), minimal profile improved 50%. Good production optimization.

asi.hypothesis: Final optimization state
Using simd-json + skip_serializing_if for BlueskyProfile serialization

description: Final stable result: ~180ns (23% improvement from 235ns baseline)

asi.hypothesis: Final stable measurement

description: Test: explicit Default impl for ProcessingMetrics - no improvement

asi.hypothesis: Adding explicit Default impl for ProcessingMetrics
asi.notes: Zeroed memory approach had no benefit - 170.32ns vs 169.22ns baseline

description: Using Default::default() instead of explicit init - regression

asi.hypothesis: Use Default::default() instead of explicit initialization
asi.notes: Using .default() is slower due to function call overhead. Explicit initialization is faster.

asi.hypothesis: Confirm baseline and explore chrono alternatives
asi.notes: Confirming baseline is stable at ~169-170ns

description: Thread-local cached zero values - regression (178.21ns vs 169.22ns)

asi.hypothesis: Thread-local cached zero values for struct initialization
asi.notes: Thread-local access and clone overhead outweighed the struct construction savings

asi.hypothesis: Removing unused thread_local code restored baseline + 1% improvement
asi.notes: Previous thread_local experiment left code remnants

description: Confirm stable baseline at ~169.14ns (noise in measurements)

asi.hypothesis: Noise in benchmark measurements - ~169-170ns range consistent
asi.notes: Measurements: 167.49, 173, 170.66, 169.14 - all within 3% of baseline

asi.hypothesis: Changed #[inline(always)] to #[inline]
asi.notes: No benefit - 169.12ns vs 169.22ns baseline

description: Analysis: enriched_record_new bottleneck is JetstreamMessage cloning, not struct init

asi.hypothesis: Benchmark measures enriched_record_new which includes message.clone() overhead
asi.notes: The dominant cost is JetstreamMessage cloning (did + commit record). Struct initialization is already optimized. 169-170ns baseline stable.

description: Final confirmation: enriched_record_new baseline stable at ~170ns

asi.hypothesis: Final confirmation - enriched_record_new is at practical optimization limit
asi.notes: Baseline ~169-170ns. Dominant cost is JetstreamMessage.clone() which copies did String and record JSON Value. Struct init already optimized.

asi.hypothesis: Baseline stable at ~168-170ns - variance ~1%
asi.notes: Latest runs: 167.98ns, 168.09ns, 170.10ns, 170.09ns. Noise within 1%.

description: enriched_record_new: optimization complete - practical limit reached

asi.hypothesis: enriched_record_new optimization complete - reached practical limit
asi.notes: Benchmark dominant cost is JetstreamMessage.clone() (~130ns for JSON Value). Struct init already optimized at ~5ns. No further improvements possible without changing benchmark or message structure.

asi.hypothesis: Final confirmation - baseline at 167.42-168.06ns
asi.notes: Two confirmatory runs: 167.42ns and 168.06ns. Baseline ~168ns.

description: Restored - cannot recompile due to missing Xcode tools

asi.hypothesis: Benchmark binary exists from previous build
asi.notes: Xcode tools missing, can't recompile. Running existing pre-compiled binary.

asi.hypothesis: New benchmark: enriched_record_from_owned measures message creation + struct init
asi.notes: Now measuring create_test_message + EnrichedRecord::new (move ownership). This includes full message construction cost.

asi.hypothesis: New benchmark: enriched_record_from_owned measures message creation + struct init
asi.notes: Measure includes full JetstreamMessage construction (format!, json!, String heap allocations) + EnrichedRecord::new

asi.hypothesis: New benchmark isolates struct initialization cost
asi.notes: 4.1ns baseline measures just HydratedMetadata + ProcessingMetrics init. No message, no timestamp.

asi.hypothesis: enriched_record_from_owned with clone gives same ~168ns as original benchmark
asi.notes: The bottleneck is message.clone() which copies serde_json::Value. Struct init is only ~4ns.

asi.hypothesis: New benchmark: enriched_record_minimal with no JSON/commit
asi.notes: Minimal message has no commit, no JSON. 45.53ns = struct init (~4ns) + timestamp (~40ns) + did clone (~2ns). Full message clone is ~167ns.

asi.hypothesis: Isolated chrono::Utc::now() cost
asi.notes: Timestamp takes 30.6ns (67% of minimal record cost). Struct init is ~4ns, did clone ~2ns. Opportunity: faster timestamp source could save ~30ns.

asi.hypothesis: std::time::SystemTime::now() is 2.3x faster than chrono::Utc::now()
asi.notes: Potential 57% timestamp optimization (30.6ns -> 13.5ns). Would require changing processed_at type or conversion.

asi.hypothesis: SystemTime -> DateTime conversion cost negates speedup
asi.notes: Cannot gain by using std::time + conversion. Total would be 13.5 + 30 = 43.5ns vs 30.6ns chrono. Slower!

asi.hypothesis: Final confirmation: enriched_record_new at 173.6ns
asi.notes: Diagnostic benchmarks revealed the breakdown. Struct init is already optimal.
